message:
style(analytics): apply rustfmt to validate.rs for idiomatic let-chain and match formatting

Reformats the analytics validation module to conform to current rustfmt rules:

- Flatten nested if-let chains into idiomatic `if let Ok(..) && let Ok(..)` chains,
  reducing nesting depth in validate_cross_track_drift by 2 levels
- Convert inline ternary-style severity assignments to block-style match arms
  for Track A (content_tokens, message_count, api_tokens, api_coverage),
  Track B (grand_total, tool_calls), and cross-track drift checks
- Expand single-line tuple destructuring to multi-line for readability in
  Track A row processing (10-element tuple)
- Normalize trailing whitespace alignment in column comments (Track B row queries)
- Reformat long assert! messages in tests to multi-line for readability
  (perf_query_guardrail, perf_breakdown_guardrail, all_ok assertion)

No behavioral changes — purely mechanical formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
